# reality-ledger — weekly advisory report

> Generated by `.github/workflows/reality-ledger.yml` (Phase 4).
> Findings below are ADVISORY ONLY. CI never blocks on this report.
>
> Per Sec MF-3 contract: `claim_source_path` is **never** included
> in this issue body — only `claim_source_sha256`. To resolve a SHA
> back to a file path, run locally:
>
>     python3 .claude/scripts/reality-ledger.py --format markdown
>
> Per ADR-101 + `_lib.redact.redact_secrets`, every `actual_evidence`
> field is pre-redacted before emission. No plaintext API keys, no
> JWT bodies, no DB connection strings should appear below.

## Summary

- detectors_run: <DETECTORS_RUN>
- total_findings: <TOTAL_FINDINGS>
- by_severity: low=<LOW> · medium=<MEDIUM> · high=<HIGH>
- by_detector:
<BY_DETECTOR_BULLETS>

## Findings

<FINDINGS_BODY>

## Triage workflow

1. For each finding, resolve `claim_source_sha256` → path locally
   (`reality-ledger.py --format markdown`).
2. Decide: wire the missing runtime read, OR amend the documented claim.
3. Open a PR; this workflow's next run will close out resolved findings
   automatically (idempotent issue per ADR-064 §15).
4. False positives → add to detector-N-negative fixture set + PR.

## Compliance

- `claim_source_path` excluded: TRUE (assertion enforced by
  `tests/fixtures/reality-ledger/issue-body-template.md` contract test).
- `actual_evidence` redacted: TRUE (`_lib.redact.redact_secrets`
  applied before render).
- Audit emit: every finding emits `reality_ledger_finding` event with
  Sec MF-3 7-field allowlist (action, ts, detector, severity, confidence,
  claim_source_sha256, finding_count_in_run).

_run_id: `<RUN_ID>`  ·  duration_ms: `<DURATION_MS>`
